title: digitalis ferruginea
intro: Digitalis ferruginea, also known as foxglove, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its tall, stately spikes of tubular flowers in shades of purple and white. The plant contains cardiac glycosides, specifically digitoxin and digoxin, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various heart conditions, including congestive heart failure and atrial fibrillation. The medicinal properties of Digitalis ferruginea have been found to lower blood pressure, increase cardiac output, and strengthen the heart's contractions, leading to improved cardiovascular health and reduced risk of heart-related complications. Herbal preparations of Digitalis ferruginea, such as tinctures, teas, and infusions, can be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional to support heart health and alleviate symptoms of heart conditions.
